visa_types.forms:
Click here to fill the visa application form online (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/de/)
The Schengen Visa Application form must be filled either in English/German.
Instructions to complete an online form:
- Click on the form link above.
- When the page opens complete the form.
- Once completed, take a printout the form.
- The complete set of application form including the last barcoded page of your application form should be presented while submitting your application. The barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and machine-readable format.
(If you are not able to fill the form online, a form filling service is available at the Visa Application Centre at an additional fee of NIS 50.)
visa_types.overview:
Overview
If you are traveling to Germany to visit family / friends, you may apply for this visa. Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
If you are traveling to Germany to visit family / friends, you may apply for this visa. Applicant will have to provide evidence that he/she falls under this category, kindly refer to the information sheet of documents required.

## Visa Fees

[deu > pse > en > Visa Fees]
Service Fee:
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Click here to download the checklist.

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4.5 cm) as per specifications.
Click here to view a sample biometric photo
Requirements:
- NEW! Should NOT be older than 6 months.
- A frontal view of the face covering 67.20 -80% (32-26mm) of the photo.
- A uniform and white-colour background.
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair or glasses frame.
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious reasons.
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the application form.

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application at the German Consulate is approximately 15 calendar days.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
Click here to fill the visa application form online (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/de/)
The Schengen Visa Application form must be filled either in English/German.
Instructions to complete an online form:
- Click on the form link above.
- When the page opens complete the form.
- Once completed, take a printout the form.
- The complete set of application form including the last barcoded page of your application form should be presented while submitting your application. The barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and machine-readable format.
(If you are not able to fill the form online, a form filling service is available at the Visa Application Centre at an additional fee of NIS 50.)

Under the Business visa category, the following cases would be applicable:

- Conference/Exhibition/Fairs
- Scientific/Research events
- Journalists
- Official delegations

visa_types.overview:
Overview
For Persons applying for Airport Transit Visa
Entry clearance requirements depend on the citizenship and resident status of the applicant (sometimes in a third country). Only a flight through one Schengen Union Country at a time is considered as a transit. In Germany, the following airports have an International Transit Area: Cologne/Bonn (transit only possible between 4:30 am and 11:00 pm), Düsseldorf (transit only possible between 6:00 am and 9:00 pm and only if the airline has arranged the transit with airport security – please check with your airline), Frankfurt/Main, Hamburg (limited time frame only 4:30 am to 11:30 pm), Munich (München).
Please check with your travel agent / airline, if your flight is going via another airport. If you leave the airport’s international transit area, i.e. change the terminal, you will have to apply for a Schengen Visa – Category C.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
For Persons applying for Airport Transit Visa
Entry clearance requirements depend on the citizenship and resident status of the applicant (sometimes in a third country). Only a flight through one Schengen Union Country at a time is considered as a transit. In Germany, the following airports have an International Transit Area: Cologne/Bonn (transit only possible between 4:30 am and 11:00 pm), Düsseldorf (transit only possible between 6:00 am and 9:00 pm and only if the airline has arranged the transit with airport security – please check with your airline), Frankfurt/Main, Hamburg (limited time frame only 4:30 am to 11:30 pm), Munich (München).
Please check with your travel agent / airline, if your flight is going via another airport. If you leave the airport’s international transit area, i.e. change the terminal, you will have to apply for a Schengen Visa – Category C.

## Visa Fees

[deu > pse > en > Visa Fees]
Service Fee:
- The applicable visa fee in NIS/ILS is as per the current exchange rate. It is subject to change without notice due to sudden foreign exchange fluctuations.
- All fees are non-refundable.

## Documents Required
Click here to download the checklist.

## Photo Specifications
Two recent biometric passport sized photographs (3.5x4.5 cm) as per specifications.
Click here to view a sample biometric photo
Requirements:
- NEW! Should NOT be older than 6 months.
- A frontal view of the face covering 67.20 -80% (32-26mm) of the photo.
- A uniform and white-colour background.
- Clear facial features and eyes – not covered by hair or glasses frame.
- Head coverings are only accepted for religious reasons.
- Do not glue or staple the pictures to the application form.

## Processing Time
The processing time for the application at the German Consulate is approximately 15 calendar days.
Please be advised that due to the above-mentioned minimum processing time, we are not able to guarantee the return of passports by specific dates for travel.
Please be sure to submit your visa application well in advance, to avoid any inconveniences.

## Download Forms
Click here to fill the visa application form online (https://videx.diplo.de/videx/visum-erfassung/de/)
The Schengen Visa Application form must be filled either in English/German.
Instructions to complete an online form:
- Click on the form link above.
- When the page opens complete the form.
- Once completed, take a printout the form.
- The complete set of application form including the last barcoded page of your application form should be presented while submitting your application. The barcode page contains the necessary data in condensed and machine-readable format.
(If you are not able to fill the form online, a form filling service is available at the Visa Application Centre at an additional fee of NIS 50.)

visa_types.name: Short-stay Schengen visa
visa_types.overview:
(For a maximum stay of 90 days per 180-day period)
If you wish to visit Germany for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Germany.
A short-stay Schengen visa allows the holder to transit through or remain in German territory and all other Schengen States for a maximum period of three months (90 days) within any period of 180 days.
Visiting more than one Schengen country?
You will have to obtain the Schengen visa from the country where the main purpose of travel lies.
When travelling to more than one Schengen country, submit your application with the country where you will be staying the longest. In case of equally long stays, you must apply with the Schengen country where you arrive first.
Short-stay Schengen visa Categories:
- Airport Transit
- Tourist
- Visiting Family & Friends
- Business
- Study
- Medical
visa_types.full_text:
(For a maximum stay of 90 days per 180-day period)
If you wish to visit Germany for a short period of time, you must obtain a visa prior to entry if you come from a country with a visa requirement for entering Germany.
A short-stay Schengen visa allows the holder to transit through or remain in German territory and all other Schengen States for a maximum period of three months (90 days) within any period of 180 days.
Visiting more than one Schengen country?
You will have to obtain the Schengen visa from the country where the main purpose of travel lies.
When travelling to more than one Schengen country, submit your application with the country where you will be staying the longest. In case of equally long stays, you must apply with the Schengen country where you arrive first.
Short-stay Schengen visa Categories:
- Airport Transit
- Tourist
- Visiting Family & Friends
- Business
- Study
- Medical
